definition: The 2025-2027 HY/leveraged loan maturity wall interacts non-linearly with the fiscal term premium channel. Issuers who locked 5Y debt at SOFR+350bp (all-in ~4.0-4.5%) face refinancing at SOFR+400-500bp (all-in ~8.5-10.0%) even after 150bp Fed cuts. For B-rated issuers at 4.0-4.5x leverage, this pushes coverage below the 1.5x distress threshold.
evidence: Refinancing costs 200-300bp above origination even after 150bp of Fed cuts
B-rated issuers at 4.0-4.5x leverage pushed below 1.5x coverage
T-bill share at 22% must normalize toward coupons adding long-end supply pressure
Arithmetic is mechanical and not directly challenged
open_questions: What is the distribution of issuers hitting the wall — concentrated or dispersed?
Can extend-and-pretend in private credit absorb enough maturities to defuse the wall?
